proof_boundary: This exact Arb countermodel blocks promotion from generic log-concavity and Berwald-Borell to adjacent-k monotonicity. It is not the zeta kernel, does not disprove zeta monotone contractions, and does not prove or disprove RH or Lambda <= 0.

rows.claim: f(y)=exp(-5*y)*1_[0,1](y) is an integrable log-concave density on [0,infinity).
rows.proof_boundary: Abstract density only; not the Riemann/Newman kernel.

rows.claim: The first Gamma-normalized Mellin contraction lies strictly between zero and one.
The second Gamma-normalized Mellin contraction lies strictly between zero and one.
The strict Arb inequality x_2<x_1 disproves any generic implication from log-concavity to adjacent-k contraction monotonicity.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact abstract countermodel gate; not a failure of the actual zeta sequence.

rows.claim: A proof of x_(k+1)>=x_k must use structure beyond kernel log-concavity and Berwald-Borell.
rows.proof_boundary: No claim against a zeta-specific recurrence, determinant identity, or saddle theorem.

summary.main_finding: The compactly supported log-concave density exp(-5y) on [0,1] has Gamma-normalized Mellin contractions x_1 and x_2 inside the Berwald-Borell upper wall but x_2<x_1. Therefore the remaining adjacent-k wall is not a generic consequence of log-concavity and needs zeta-specific structure.
